description: The Benson Syndicate was a 19th-century criminal organization in the western United States that fraudulently executed or fabricated cadastral land surveys for the U.S. General Land Office. Led by John A. Benson, it operated from the mid-1870s to 1898, primarily in California with headquarters in San Francisco. The syndicate infiltrated government positions, including California Surveyors General, to award contracts and approve fraudulent surveys, affecting nearly 20 million acres. Its downfall began in 1885 when ground inspections were instituted.
